what_it_does: A deep-read document assembling the per-surface design-intent layer for the Company's application: a criterion-by-criterion depth pass on the Interactive Addressed Surface (the studio end-state spec), an architecture-level survey of the other surfaces (canvas, IA, inbox, walkthrough, twin, settings, etc.), a treatment of the Sequences primitive, and a surface relationship map placing the Information Architecture as the parent container. Every claim is tagged DESIGNED / BUILT / DESIGNED-checkbox / INFERRED.
observations: Part 1 walks every Interactive-Addressed-Surface criterion (S0-S5, F0-F9, I1-I7, R1-R2, B1-B3, L1-L10, L2) with designed shape, test name, and status verified against intent-studio.md section 5 (code-truth on main) rather than the Completion Criteria checkboxes.

States the load-bearing interaction model: click INDICATES + CONSENTS (not click=execute); the RHM proposes in chat; action runs only through governance + see-and-approve, with tier resolved by address (I4) not verb.

Gives a BUILT-on-main vs DESIGNED summary cut: I6/I7 (annotate/attach_chat), F1-presentation (set_presentation_pref), S3 (resolve_scope), L4 (ingest_comment), L3/L5 substrate, address_help, up_translate, _registry_ui_target are BUILT; the spine (S0/S1/S2/F4), FORM rebuild (F0-F9), interaction loop (I1-I4), R1/R2, and L1/L2 wire are net-new.

Part 2 surveys ~17 surfaces (studio, IA, canvas, portals, workshop, RHM presence, walkthrough, presence dial, twin, inbox, build review, replay, wire states, self-mod, model fleet, activity/SSE, settings) with intent + built reality + how each composes the primitives.

Part 3 frames the Sequences primitive as one relational loop resolve->present/work->persist->next/trigger recurring across the engine, the walkthrough organ, and the collective cognition, with three citations.

Part 4 maps the Information Architecture as the parent container that places every other surface, with an ASCII grid layout and a relationship graph; the threading principle is simultaneity (the same ui:// address visible on board, inspector, and RHM pane at once).

Part 5 is a source ledger; includes an explicit honesty note distinguishing Collective Cognition (the context-resolution spine, used here) from Concurrent Cognition (the staged-stream runtime, out of scope, no doc in this corpus).
standouts: The methodology correction: it maps each criterion against code-truth (intent-studio section 5) not the all-checkbox Completion Criteria, and flags a resolved source-conflict where the 4-day-newer reading supersedes the Research Synthesis on S3.
The IA-as-parent / co-resolution-surface insight: the IA is not a navigation shell but a surface where every zone re-resolves to the same locus simultaneously (R1/R2 rendered as spatial layout).

The Sequences primitive as the single mechanic underlying engine, human-review organ, and cognition, so new capability is registering a station on the existing loop rather than building a new pipeline.
The honesty contract with four tags and the explicit Collective-vs-Concurrent Cognition disambiguation.
conventions: Uses a four-level honesty tag system (DESIGNED / BUILT / DESIGNED-checkbox / INFERRED) and present-tense claims only for BUILT, matching Tim's verify-before-claiming law.
Cites file:line (suite.py) for BUILT claims and names the acceptance test for each criterion.
Names sources per-section and carries a source ledger plus an explicit not-verified list flagged for a next wave.
concerns: Self-flags that many surface rows (replay, twin, and mockups 2.4/2.5/2.6/2.9/2.11-2.16) are DESIGNED from secondary mention with the underlying mockup files not actually read; the per-surface intent for those is lighter than Part 1.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the design-intent atlas for the whole surface layer, deepest on the Interactive Addressed Surface, and its load-bearing claims are the click-indicates-consents model, the IA-as-parent co-resolution layout, and the Sequences-as-universal-loop frame.
questions: Has the rebuilt in-app studio (superseding the standalone HTML portal) been built on any branch yet, or does only the supersession note exist?
Do the secondary-mention surfaces (replay C5, twin B6, the unread mockups) have their own intent docs that would refine the lighter rows here?
purpose_vs_actual: Match. Stated as the per-surface skeletons + designed intent pack at full scope, and it delivers per-criterion intent for the keystone surface, a survey of the rest, the underlying primitive, and the relationship map.
apparent_themes: Click indicates and consents, never executes; governance by address-tier
Address-keyed co-resolution (the same locus threaded simultaneously through all zones)
Compose on already-built primitives rather than reinventing them (the studio's whole disconnect)
One recurring loop (Sequences) as the universal substrate for engine, review, and cognition
Assemble surfaces from registries, never author against them
intention_signals: Trying to give the Claude Design effort an evidence-tagged, code-checked map of what each surface is meant to be and how they relate, so the design composes on the built primitives and the IA-as-parent structure rather than rebuilding capability or authoring bolt-on surfaces.
novelty: The synthesis that the IA is a co-resolution surface (not a menu) and that the Sequences primitive is simultaneously the engine, the human-review organ, and cognition, making capability-addition a matter of registering a station on one loop.
contribution: Contributes the per-criterion designed-intent + verified build-status for the Interactive Addressed Surface, a composed survey of every other surface, the Sequences-primitive frame, and the IA-as-parent relationship map with layout; a fused system keeps this as the surface-design-intent reference that names what each surface is for and how it relates to the rest.
summary_for_embedding: A deep-read design-intent atlas for the Company's surfaces: a per-criterion code-verified pass on the Interactive Addressed Surface, a survey of the canvas/IA/inbox/walkthrough/twin/settings surfaces, the Sequences-primitive frame, and a relationship map placing the Information Architecture as the parent co-resolution container, with DESIGNED/BUILT/INFERRED honesty tags.
intention_for_embedding: To give the design effort an evidence-tagged map of each surface's intent and their relationships so it composes on built primitives and the IA-as-parent structure instead of reinventing or bolting on surfaces.
